purpose: Current production bundle debt ceiling. The v3.51.22 CSV import client-integrity boundary measures 4,570,199 raw / 1,310,246 gzip JavaScript bytes on the clean source head and 4,570,181 / 1,310,205 on the hosted PR merge candidate. Against the committed v3.51.20 measured artifact (4,554,710 / 1,304,738), the larger source measurement is +15,489 raw / +5,508 gzip (under 0.43%) across the v3.51.21 server-error and v3.51.22 client-integrity boundaries: deterministic response validation, versioned guidance detection, source-index preservation, last-selection-wins parsing, duplicate-submit and mapping-collision guards, bounded error-body reads, accessibility semantics, and localized recovery copy. No database, Edge wire contract, CSS, dependency or speculative request timeout was added. The total ceilings retain only the existing 128-byte raw and 279-byte gzip cross-platform/SHA allowances; largest gzip retains the existing 35-byte allowance. Further increases require an explicit reviewed baseline update.
